lectureSummary: Pre-mRNA processing includes 5-prime capping, splicing, 3-prime cleavage, and polyadenylation, producing mature transcripts with different stability and translation potential.
testableFacts: Alternative splicing changes transcript isoforms without changing genomic DNA
Polyadenylation affects transcript stability and translation
diagramCandidates: Pre-mRNA processing strip with cap, introns, and poly(A) tail
misconceptions: Treating splicing as a DNA-level editing event
